risks: No Microsoft.Insights/autoscaleSettings resource defined for the VMSS despite the user goal explicitly requesting autoscaling
No NSG resources defined for any subnet; SQL Managed Instance requires an NSG on its delegated subnet (service-aided configuration can auto-create, but explicit definition is recommended)
No route table resource for the SQL MI subnet; SQL MI connectivity architecture requires an associated route table
No load balancer resource for the VMSS presentation tier, limiting traffic distribution and health probing
UbuntuServer 18.04-LTS image is end-of-life (April 2023) and may be unavailable or unsupported in the marketplace
No availability zones configured for VMs or VMSS, reducing resilience against datacenter failures
SQL MI uses SQL authentication (administratorLogin) without Microsoft Entra ID authentication configured
No monitoring or diagnostics resources (Log Analytics workspace, Application Insights) included in the plan
No Key Vault resource for secrets management; admin credentials are defined inline
Application tier VMs have no load balancer or availability set, limiting high-availability guarantees
correctionsRecommended: Add a Microsoft.Insights/autoscaleSettings resource targeting the VMSS with CPU-based or schedule-based scaling rules
Add Microsoft.Network/networkSecurityGroups resources for each subnet with appropriate inbound/outbound rules
Add a Microsoft.Network/routeTables resource associated with the SQL MI subnet
Add a Microsoft.Network/loadBalancers (Standard SKU) in front of the VMSS for the presentation tier
Update the VM image reference from UbuntuServer 18.04-LTS to a supported version such as 0001-com-ubuntu-server-jammy 22_04-lts-gen2
Configure availability zones for the VMSS and application-tier VMs to improve fault tolerance
Add a Microsoft.OperationalInsights/workspaces resource and Microsoft.Insights/components for centralized monitoring
Add a Microsoft.KeyVault/vaults resource and reference it for SQL MI admin credentials and other secrets
Consider adding a load balancer or availability set for the application-tier VMs
Consider enabling Microsoft Entra ID authentication for SQL Managed Instance alongside SQL authentication
